name: dialect-abstraction
question: How to handle PostgreSQL vs SQLite differences?
decision: Define SqlDialect interface: { name: string, escapeIdentifier(name): string, mapType(jsonType, format): string, supportsForeignKeys: boolean, supportsCheckConstraints: boolean }. Implementations: PostgresDialect (UUID, TIMESTAMPTZ, BOOLEAN, ALTER TABLE FKs), SqliteDialect (TEXT fallbacks, INTEGER for bool, inline FKs)
rationale: Interface allows adding dialects without changing core logic. Type mapping is dialect-specific (UUID vs TEXT). FK syntax differs (ALTER TABLE vs inline). Analysis found escapeIdentifier pattern in SqlBackend.
